name: Canal de Orellana — circular desde Torrefresneda
region_intro: Jogging around Guareña offers a variety of routes through the flat plains and gentle hills of Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by agricultural landscapes, river systems, and reservoirs, providing diverse scenery for runners. Trails often follow canals and reservoir edges, with some routes venturing into slightly more elevated areas like the Sierra de la Bóveda. The terrain is generally accessible, with options ranging from easy, flat paths to more challenging, longer running routes. Best jogging routes around Guareña The most popular jogging route is Cornalvo Reservoir Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views along the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Silo de Santa Amalia by La Morra, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This route traverses open landscapes, providing a straightforward running experience. Local runners also love the El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo, a 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail leading through reservoir surroundings, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Jogging around Guareña is defined by reservoir paths, canal-side running routes, and open agricultural land. The network offers options for easy jogging routes and more challenging long-distance running. The routes in Guareña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 2 reviews. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Guareña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Guareña?
A: There are over 40 running routes in the Guareña area, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from easy, flat paths to more challenging long-distance runs.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Guareña?
A: Yes, Guareña offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners. The terrain is generally accessible, with many paths following canals and reservoir edges. There are 2 routes specifically classified as easy, and 21 moderate routes that can also be suitable depending on your fitness level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Guareña?
A: Jogging routes in Guareña primarily traverse the flat plains and gentle hills of Extremadura. You'll encounter agricultural landscapes, river systems, and reservoirs. Many paths follow canals and reservoir edges, with some routes venturing into slightly more elevated areas like the Sierra de la Bóveda.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Guareña region?
A: Yes, the Guareña area features several circular running routes. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option leading through reservoir surroundings. Another circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284530340/canal-de-orellana-circular-desde-torrefresneda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Orellana — circular desde Torrefresneda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see while running near Guareña?
A: Many running routes in the Guareña area offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sights in the wider region include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5725452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theater and Castle of Medellín&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medellín Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Guareña?
A: The running routes in Guareña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Over 250 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic reservoir paths and open agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of lakes or reservoirs?
A: Absolutely. The region is characterized by its river systems and reservoirs, providing excellent opportunities for lakeside runs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering beautiful views along the reservoir. You can also find routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6059030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Muelas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6067846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Voluntário&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes that are suitable for families with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' classifications aren't always available, the generally flat and accessible paths along canals and reservoir edges are often suitable for families. Look for the 'easy' rated routes, which tend to be shorter and have less elevation gain, making them more manageable for younger runners.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Guareña?
A: The best time for running in Guareña is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late evening runs preferable. Winters are generally mild, but it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts for optimal conditions.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Guareña offers several longer and more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt; is an 18.3 km trail with significant elevation changes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284530340/canal-de-orellana-circular-desde-torrefresneda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Orellana — circular desde Torrefresneda&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 15 km.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while jogging in Guareña?
A: The natural landscapes around Guareña, particularly near the reservoirs and river systems, provide habitats for various wildlife. While not guaranteed, you may spot local bird species and other small animals, especially during quieter times of the day on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Next to the Canal de Orellana we find this path that runs alongside the waters and where it is surrounded by crops on one side and on the other, by the forests of the slopes of the Natural Park of Cornalvo.
In the passage of the Canal de Orellana near what are called the Cerros Verdes there is a narrowing of the canal forming a narrow passage.
